limitations: R1 side chain classification based on SMILES analysis + literature
Cross-reactivity rates from limited prospective studies
R2 side chain role excluded (debated in literature, PMID 29017833)
Carbapenems: cross-reactivity data limited to low risk (structural basis)
PMID 19489917 excluded: not in verified database

evidence_notes.stevenson_2026: Retrospective audit, n=212, 2 Australian tertiary hospitals, 2016-2021. Strong support for R1 model. Limitation: limited non-cefazolin confirmed allergies.
evidence_notes.hutten_2025: Systematic comparison of 4 cross-reactivity tables covering 51 BAs. Found 36% disharmony, highlighting need for standardization. No new clinical data but critical for interpretation.
evidence_notes.clinical_predictors: Anaphylaxis (OR 6.05) and IV route (OR 9.68) are significant predictors of positive ST (Stevenson 2026).

r1_groups.name: aminobenzyl
r1_groups.description: D-alpha-aminophenylacetyl side chain with unsubstituted benzene ring

r1_groups.name: aminothiazolyl-carboxypropoxyimino
r1_groups.description: 2-aminothiazol-4-yl with tert-butyl carboxylic acid oxyimino substituent

r1_groups.name: benzyl
r1_groups.description: Phenylacetyl side chain without amino group

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: side-chain similarities or identities
shared side chains
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: confirmatory challenge data are lacking

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: distinct side chains
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: do not have any skin test cross-reactivity

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: side chains different from those of penicillins
side chains different from those of penicillins
shared antigenic determinants
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: T-cell crossreactivity observed

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: unique R1 side chain - mechanism unclear, possible false positive or cosensitization
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: 5/49 cefazolin-allergic patients without PAL had positive penicillin ST; none confirmed by OPC. May represent false positives (ref: 7.3% false positive rate in similar studies).

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: different R1 side chains
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: No cross-reactivity found outside R1 side-chain groups in 58 confirmed cephalosporin-allergic patients. Reinforces R1 model.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: shared R1 side chain (aminobenzyl group)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: 1 cefalexin-allergic patient reacted to amoxicillin OPC with urticaria. Confirms R1 side-chain cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: multiple sensitizations, possible broad BL reactivity
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Patients with multiple BL allergy labels warrant more caution. Avoid direct challenges without preceding skin tests.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Trubiano: safe; Romano & Zagursky: identical R1 ring
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Clinically relevant discrepancy between major cross-reactivity references. Exercise caution.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Trubiano: safe; Romano & Zagursky: similar R1 side chain
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Discrepancy between references. Conservative approach: treat as potential cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Non-identical R1 side chain but clinical cross-reactivity reported (aminothiazolyl group variants)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017: cefuroxime (furyl-methoxyimino) shares partial aminothiazolyl-methoxyimino structural motif with ceftriaxone/cefotaxime/cefepime group. Clinical cross-reactivity documented despite non-identical R1.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Non-identical R1 but shared methoxyimino moiety
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Partial structural similarity in methoxyimino substituent. Clinical cross-reactivity reported.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Non-identical R1 but shared methoxyimino moiety
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017 groups cefuroxime with cefepime/cefotaxime/ceftriaxone/ceftazidime for some clinical cross-reactivity evidence.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Non-identical R1; ceftazidime has carboxypropoxyimino vs furyl-methoxyimino
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Grouped together by Trubiano but weaker structural basis. Different oxyimino substituents.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Different R1 side chains
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017: 3rd-gen cephalosporins can be used in patients with non-immediate or non-life-threatening penicillin allergy.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Different ring structure (5-membered with carbon replacing sulfur)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017: Carbapenems can be used in penicillin-allergic patients. Cross-reactivity ≤1%. Risk/benefit assessment for life-threatening penicillin allergy.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Monobactam: no fused ring, sulfonate instead of carboxylate
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017: Monobactams can be used in patients with any history of penicillin allergy.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Piperacillin R1 (ureido-aminobenzyl, G14) shares aminobenzyl backbone with G1/G2
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Piperacillin is a ureido-penicillin with aminobenzyl-derived R1. Cross-reactivity with aminopenicillins reported. Skin testing recommended before use in ampicillin/amoxicillin-allergic patients.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared penicillin core (beta-lactam + thiazolidine ring). Different R1 side chains but core-mediated cross-reactivity possible.
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Penicillin class cross-reactivity via shared core structure. In confirmed penicillin allergy (SPT+), other penicillins including piperacillin should be avoided (Trubiano 2017).

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Identical R1 side chain (aminothiazolyl-carboxypropoxyimino, G4)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Well-established cross-reactivity. Both share identical R1 (G4). Avoid ceftazidime in aztreonam-allergic patients and vice versa.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared glycopeptide core structure
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Case report: glycopeptide cross-reactivity risk highlighted. Close hematological monitoring recommended.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Lipoglycopeptide derived from glycopeptide backbone
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Case series: 10 vancomycin-allergic patients received dalbavancin (4 had Type I IgE history). Limited data on cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Teicoplanin-specific T-cells cross-react with daptomycin (lipopeptide). CD8+, HLA class I restricted.
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: CASE REPORT: Teicoplanin-specific T-cells showed proliferation and cytotoxicity (granzyme B, perforin, FasL) with daptomycin. Exercise caution in teicoplanin DRESS patients.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Cosensitization (not classical R1-based cross-reactivity)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: CASE REPORT: Pediatric cefotaxime-induced DRESS with cosensitization to teicoplanin and cross-reactivity to other cephalosporins.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Different ring structure but shared beta-lactam core
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Cross-reactivity ≤1%. Carbapenems generally safe in penicillin-allergic patients. Risk/benefit for life-threatening allergy.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Different ring structure but shared beta-lactam core
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Cross-reactivity ≤1%. Imipenem historically slightly higher concern than meropenem but still low.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared quinolone core, different substituents
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Patients with ciprofloxacin HSR may tolerate moxifloxacin. 9.5% reaction rate on provocation.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared quinolone core
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Low cross-reactivity. Levofloxacin had lowest reaction rate (2.2%) among tested FQ alternatives.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Structural similarity suspected between aminoglycoside and LMWH
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: CASE REPORT: Positive patch tests to enoxaparin and tinzaparin in neomycin contact allergy patients. Unusual cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Lipoglycopeptide, vancomycin derivative
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Vancomycin DRESS associated with HLA-A*32:01. Telavancin is a vancomycin derivative - theoretical cross-reactivity risk.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared penicillin core (beta-lactam + thiazolidine ring), different R1
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Penicillin class cross-reactivity via shared core. Confirmed penicillin allergy: avoid all penicillins.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared penicillin core, different R1
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Penicillin class cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared penicillin core, different R1
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Penicillin class cross-reactivity.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: In vitro data: shared but non-identical R1 proposed cross-reactivity (Zagursky). Disputed by other authors.
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Zagursky: in vitro cross-reactivity between penicillin G, cefoxitin, and cephalothin based on shared but non-identical R1. Not universally accepted.

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Identical R1 side chain (thienyl-acetyl, G8)
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Both share identical thienyl-acetyl R1 (G8). Cephalothin (1st gen, discontinued in many countries) and cefoxitin (cephamycin).

literature_confirmed_pairs.structural_basis: Shared R2 side chain (identical R2), non-identical R1
literature_confirmed_pairs.clinical_note: Trubiano 2017: cefoxitin-cefotaxime share identical R2 side chain. Cross-reactivity based on R2 is less established than R1.

prediction_rules.notes: IgE-mediated cross-reactivity primarily driven by R1 side chain identity (PMID 33500632)
G1(aminobenzyl) vs G2(para-OH-aminobenzyl): structural variant, lower but possible cross-reactivity
Different R1 side chains account for absence of cross-reactivity (PMID 32145403)
Cross-reactivity from beta-lactam ring alone is infrequent in IgE-mediated allergy (PMID 33500632)

36% disharmony found between 4 major cross-reactivity tables (Romano, Trubiano, Zagursky, SWAB). 272/394 discrepancies were clinically relevant (different safe vs unsafe advice). Always check multiple sources.
In 58 confirmed cephalosporin-allergic patients, 0/58 had positive ST to unrelated R1 cephalosporins and 0/7 reacted on OPC. Strong evidence for R1 model safety.

Trubiano 2017: Some non-identical R1 pairs show clinical cross-reactivity, particularly cefuroxime (G6 furyl-methoxyimino) with aminothiazolyl-methoxyimino group (G3). R1 similarity rather than identity may drive some cross-reactivity.

predict_function_logic: Same R1 group → HIGH | G1↔G2 → MODERATE | Different → LOW/SAFE

literature_monitoring.query: ((beta-lactam*[Title/Abstract] OR cephalosporin*[Title/Abstract] OR penicillin*[Title/Abstract] OR carbapenem*[Title/Abstract] OR monobactam*[Title/Abstract] OR antibiotic*[Title/Abstract])) AND (cross-react*[Title/Abstract] OR hypersensitiv*[Title/Abstract] OR allergy[Title/Abstract] OR anaphylaxis[Title/Abstract]) AND (case report[Publication Type] OR clinical study[Publication Type] OR cohort[Title/Abstract] OR trial[Title/Abstract])
